task_completion.description: The agent successfully loads the LIDC-IDRI chest CT volume into 3D Slicer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D Slicer interface is visible with the 'Data' or 'Volumes' module loaded, and the slice views (Red, Yellow, Green) display cross-sectional CT images rather than being empty/black.

task_completion.description: The agent applies Lung Window settings and navigates to show lung parenchyma details.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The slice view displays the lungs as dark fields with distinct white vascular/bronchial structures (web-like patterns). The Window/Level values in the UI (if visible) should be approx Width 1500, Level -500.

task_completion.description: The agent applies Soft Tissue Window settings to visualize the mediastinum.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The slice view displays soft tissues (heart, muscles, fat) in varying shades of grey. The lungs appear black/featureless. The Window/Level values in the UI (if visible) should be approx Width 400, Level 40.

task_completion.description: The agent applies Bone Window settings to visualize skeletal structures.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The slice view displays bones (ribs, spine) as bright white with visible internal trabecular structure, while soft tissue is dark. The Window/Level values in the UI (if visible) should be approx Width 2000, Level 400.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the required screenshots (lung_window.png, soft_tissue_window.png, bone_window.png).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent using the 'Save Screenshot' dialog in Slicer, or a file explorer window listing the three specific PNG files in the ~/Documents/SlicerData/LIDC/ directory.

task_completion.description: The agent creates and saves the window_level_report.json file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent is seen using a text editor to write a JSON object containing 'lung_window', 'soft_tissue_window', and 'bone_window' keys, or a file explorer shows the json file saved in the correct directory.

integrity.description: The agent uses the 3D Slicer GUI to adjust Window/Level settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse interacting with the Volumes module sliders, the Window/Level toolbar, or dragging on the slice view, rather than running a script to process images in the background.

integrity.description: The output images are generated from the actual Slicer session.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visual content of the saved screenshots matches the state of the Slicer viewport observed during the session (e.g., same slice position or layout).

integrity.description: The values in the report correspond to the settings used in the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the report content is visible, the values written (e.g., W: 1500) match the values seen in the Slicer interface during the visualization steps.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Window/Level ranges: Lung (Width 1200-1800, Level -700 to -400); Soft Tissue (Width 300-500, Level 20-60); Bone (Width 1500-2500, Level 200-600). Lung window visually emphasizes air-filled spaces (black) and vessels (white). Soft tissue window emphasizes muscle/fat (grey). Bone window emphasizes high-density bone (bright white).
